verses.latin_vulgate: Fili mi, custodi sermones meos,  et præcepta mea reconde tibi.  Fili,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭נִי שְׁמֹ֣ר אֲמָרָ֑י וּ֝מִצְוֺתַ֗י תִּצְפֹּ֥ן אִתָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: My son, keep my words, and lay up my precepts with thee. Son,
verses.finnish_biblia: Poikani! kätke minun sanani, ja pidä tykönäs minun käskyni.
verses.king_james: My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: serva mandata mea, et vives ;  et legem meam quasi pupillam oculi tui :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמֹ֣ר מִצְוֺתַ֣י וֶחְיֵ֑ה וְ֝תוֹרָתִ֗י כְּאִישׁ֥וֹן עֵינֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Keep my commandments, and thou shalt live: and my law as the apple of thy eye:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ota vaari minun käskyistäni, niin sinä elää saat, ja minun laistani, niinkuin silmäs terästä.
verses.king_james: Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.

verses.latin_vulgate: liga eam in digitis tuis,  scribe illam in tabulis cordis tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קׇשְׁרֵ֥ם עַל־אֶצְבְּעֹתֶ֑יךָ כׇּ֝תְבֵ֗ם עַל־ל֥וּחַ לִבֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Bind it upon thy fingers, write it upon the tables of thy heart.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sido ne sormiis, kirjoita ne sydämes tauluun.
verses.king_james: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dic sapientiæ : Soror mea es,  et prudentiam voca amicam tuam :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱמֹ֣ר לַ֭חׇכְמָה אֲחֹ֣תִי אָ֑תְּ וּ֝מֹדָ֗ע לַבִּינָ֥ה תִקְרָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Say to wisdom: Thou art my sister: and call prudence thy friend,
verses.finnish_biblia: Sano viiisaudelle: sinä olet sisareni, ja kutsu toimi ystäväkses,
verses.king_james: Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister; and call understanding thy kinswoman:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut custodiant te a muliere extranea,  et ab aliena quæ verba sua dulcia facit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִ֭שְׁמׇרְךָ מֵאִשָּׁ֣ה זָרָ֑ה מִ֝נׇּכְרִיָּ֗ה אֲמָרֶ֥יהָ הֶחֱלִֽיקָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: That she may keep thee from the woman that is not thine, and from the stranger who sweeteneth her words.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että hän sinua varjelis muukalaisesta vaimosta, ja vieraasta, jonka sanat sileät ovat.
verses.king_james: That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.

verses.latin_vulgate: De fenestra enim domus meæ  per cancellos prospexi,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֭י בְּחַלּ֣וֹן בֵּיתִ֑י בְּעַ֖ד אֶשְׁנַבִּ֣י נִשְׁקָֽפְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I looked out of the window of my house through the lattice,
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minä kurkistelin huoneeni akkunasta läpi häkin.
verses.king_james: For at the window of my house I looked through my casement,

verses.latin_vulgate: et video parvulos ;  considero vecordem juvenem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֵ֤רֶא בַפְּתָאיִ֗ם אָ֘בִ֤ינָה בַבָּנִ֗ים נַ֣עַר חֲסַר־לֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I see little ones, I behold a foolish young man,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja näin tyhmän nuorukaisen taitamattomain seassa, ja äkkäsin hänen poikain joukossa;
verses.king_james: And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,

verses.latin_vulgate: qui transit per plateam juxta angulum  et prope viam domus illius graditur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹבֵ֣ר בַּ֭שּׁוּק אֵ֣צֶל פִּנָּ֑הּ וְדֶ֖רֶךְ בֵּיתָ֣הּ יִצְעָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who passeth through the street by the corner, and goeth nigh the way of her house,
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka käveli kaduilla hänen nurkkainsa taitse, ja asteli sitä tietä, joka meni hänen huoneeseensa.
verses.king_james: Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,

verses.latin_vulgate: in obscuro, advesperascente die,  in noctis tenebris et caligine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנֶֽשֶׁף־בְּעֶ֥רֶב י֑וֹם בְּאִישׁ֥וֹן לַ֝֗יְלָה וַאֲפֵלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the dark when it grows late, in the darkness and obscurity of the night.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hämärissä, kuin päivä ehtoolla oli, ja jo yö ja pimiä tuli:
verses.king_james: In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce occurrit illi mulier ornatu meretricio,  præparata ad capiendas animas :  garrula et vaga,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִנֵּ֣ה אִ֭שָּׁה לִקְרָאת֑וֹ שִׁ֥ית ז֝וֹנָ֗ה וּנְצֻ֥רַת לֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And behold a woman meeteth him in harlot's attire, prepared to deceive souls: talkative and wandering,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, häntä kohtasi vaimo porton vaatteilla, kavala,
verses.king_james: And, behold, there met him a woman with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: quietis impatiens,  nec valens in domo consistere pedibus suis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹמִיָּ֣ה הִ֣יא וְסֹרָ֑רֶת בְּ֝בֵיתָ֗הּ לֹא־יִשְׁכְּנ֥וּ רַגְלֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Not bearing to be quiet, not able to abide still at home,
verses.finnish_biblia: Tuima ja hillimätöin, jonka jalat ei pysyneet huoneessansa.
verses.king_james: (She is loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house:

verses.latin_vulgate: nunc foris, nunc in plateis,  nunc juxta angulos insidians.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פַּ֤עַם ׀ בַּח֗וּץ פַּ֥עַם בָּרְחֹב֑וֹת וְאֵ֖צֶל כׇּל־פִּנָּ֣ה תֶאֱרֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now abroad, now in the streets, now lying in wait near the corners.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nyt on hän ulkona, nyt kaduilla, ja väijyy joka nurkassa.
verses.king_james: Now is she without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Apprehensumque deosculatur juvenem,  et procaci vultu blanditur, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֶחֱזִ֣יקָה בּ֭וֹ וְנָ֣שְׁקָה לּ֑וֹ הֵעֵ֥זָה פָ֝נֶ֗יהָ וַתֹּ֣אמַר לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And catching the young man, she kisseth him, and with an impudent face, flattereth, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän otti hänen kiinni, ja suuta antoi hänen ja sanoi häpeemättä:
verses.king_james: So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him,

verses.latin_vulgate: Victimas pro salute vovi ;  hodie reddidi vota mea :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זִבְחֵ֣י שְׁלָמִ֣ים עָלָ֑י הַ֝יּ֗וֹם שִׁלַּ֥מְתִּי נְדָרָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I vowed victims for prosperity, this day I have paid my vows.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen tehnyt kiitosuhrin, ja olen tätäpänä täyttänyt lupaukseni:
verses.king_james: I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.

verses.latin_vulgate: idcirco egressa sum in occursum tuum,  desiderans te videre, et reperi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־כֵּ֭ן יָצָ֣אתִי לִקְרָאתֶ֑ךָ לְשַׁחֵ֥ר פָּ֝נֶ֗יךָ וָאֶמְצָאֶֽךָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore I am come out to meet thee, desirous to see thee, and I have found thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden olen minä tullut sinua vastaan, varhain sinua etsimään, ja olen nyt sinut löytänyt.
verses.king_james: Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intexui funibus lectulum meum ;  stravi tapetibus pictis ex Ægypto :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֭רְבַדִּים רָבַ֣דְתִּי עַרְשִׂ֑י חֲ֝טֻב֗וֹת אֵט֥וּן מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have woven my bed with cords, I have covered it with painted tapestry, brought from Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen koreasti valmistanut vuoteeni Egyptin kirjavalla vaatteella,
verses.king_james: I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved works, with fine linen of Egypt.

verses.latin_vulgate: aspersi cubile meum myrrha,  et aloë, et cinnamomo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֥פְתִּי מִשְׁכָּבִ֑י מֹ֥ר אֲ֝הָלִ֗ים וְקִנָּמֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja olen hyvänhajuiseksi tehnyt kammioni mirhamilla, aloella ja kanelilla.
verses.king_james: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veni, inebriemur uberibus,  et fruamur cupitis amplexibus donec illucescat dies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְכָ֤ה נִרְוֶ֣ה דֹ֭דִים עַד־הַבֹּ֑קֶר נִ֝תְעַלְּסָ֗ה בׇּאֳהָבִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Come, let us be inebriated with the breasts, and let us enjoy the desired embraces, till the day appear.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tule, harjoittakaamme kyllin hekumaa aamuun asti, ja huvitelkaammme meitämme keskenämme rakkaudella.
verses.king_james: Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est enim vir in domo sua :  abiit via longissima :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֵ֣ין הָאִ֣ישׁ בְּבֵית֑וֹ הָ֝לַ֗ךְ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ מֵרָחֽוֹק׃
verses.douay_rheims: For my husband is not at home, he is gone a very long journey.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä mies ei ole kotona: hän on pitkälle matkalle mennyt.
verses.king_james: For the goodman is not at home, he is gone a long journey:

verses.latin_vulgate: sacculum pecuniæ secum tulit ;  in die plenæ lunæ reversurus est in domum suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צְֽרוֹר־הַ֭כֶּסֶף לָקַ֣ח בְּיָד֑וֹ לְי֥וֹם הַ֝כֵּ֗סֶא יָבֹ֥א בֵיתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He took with him a bag of money: he will return home the day of the full moon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on ottanut rahasäkin myötänsä, ja tulee kotia äsken määrätyllä päivällä.
verses.king_james: He hath taken a bag of money with him, and will come home at the day appointed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Irretivit eum multis sermonibus,  et blanditiis labiorum protraxit illum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֭טַּתּוּ בְּרֹ֣ב לִקְחָ֑הּ בְּחֵ֥לֶק שְׂ֝פָתֶ֗יהָ תַּדִּיחֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: She entangled him with many words, and drew him away with the flattery of her lips.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin hän houkutteli monelöla sanalla, ja vaati häntä makialla puheella.
verses.king_james: With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Statim eam sequitur quasi bos ductus ad victimam,  et quasi agnus lasciviens,  et ignorans quod ad vincula stultus trahatur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֤וֹלֵ֥ךְ אַחֲרֶ֗יהָ פִּ֫תְאֹ֥ם כְּ֭שׁוֹר אֶל־טֶ֣בַח יָבֹ֑א וּ֝כְעֶ֗כֶס אֶל־מוּסַ֥ר אֱוִֽיל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Immediately he followeth her as an ox led to be a victim, and as a lamb playing the wanton, and not knowing that he is drawn like a fool to bonds,
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän meni hänen kanssansa nopiasti, niinkuin teurastettava härkä, ja niinkuin jalkapuuhun, jolla tyhmät rangoitaan.
verses.king_james: He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks;

verses.latin_vulgate: donec transfigat sagitta jecur ejus,  velut si avis festinet ad laqueum,  et nescit quod de periculo animæ illius agitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֤ד יְפַלַּ֪ח חֵ֡ץ כְּֽבֵד֗וֹ כְּמַהֵ֣ר צִפּ֣וֹר אֶל־פָּ֑ח וְלֹא־יָ֝דַ֗ע כִּֽי־בְנַפְשׁ֥וֹ הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Till the arrow pierce his liver: as if a bird should make haste to the snare, and knoweth not that his life is in danger.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siihenasti kuin hän nuolilla ampuu hänen maksansa lävitse: ja niinkuin lintu itsensä kiiruhtaa paulaan, ja ei tiedä sitä hengellensä vaaralliseksi.
verses.king_james: Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc ergo, fili mi, audi me,  et attende verbis oris mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֣ה בָ֭נִים שִׁמְעוּ־לִ֑י וְ֝הַקְשִׁ֗יבוּ לְאִמְרֵי־פִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now, therefore, my son, hear me, and attend to the words of my mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kuulkaat siis minua, minun lapseni, ja ottakaat vaari minun suuni sanoista.
verses.king_james: Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne abstrahatur in viis illius mens tua,  neque decipiaris semitis ejus ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־יֵ֣שְׂטְ אֶל־דְּרָכֶ֣יהָ לִבֶּ֑ךָ אַל־תֵּ֝֗תַע בִּנְתִיבוֹתֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let not thy mind be drawn away in her ways: neither be thou deceived with her paths.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älköön sydämes poiketko hänen tiellensä: älä salli sinuas vietellä hänen retkillensä.
verses.king_james: Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.

verses.latin_vulgate: multos enim vulneratos dejecit,  et fortissimi quique interfecti sunt ab ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־רַבִּ֣ים חֲלָלִ֣ים הִפִּ֑ילָה וַ֝עֲצֻמִ֗ים כׇּל־הֲרֻגֶֽיהָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For she hath cast down many wounded, and the strongest have been slain by her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä hän on monta haavoittanut ja langettanut, ja kaikkinaiset voimalliset ovat häneltä tapetut.
verses.king_james: For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viæ inferi domus ejus,  penetrantes in interiora mortis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דַּרְכֵ֣י שְׁא֣וֹל בֵּיתָ֑הּ יֹ֝רְד֗וֹת אֶל־חַדְרֵי־מָֽוֶת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Her house is the way to hell, reaching even to the inner chambers of death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen huoneensa ovat helvetin tiet, jotka menevät alas kuoleman kammioihin.
verses.king_james: Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.
